ddls:
CREATE TABLE "accounts" (
  "account_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each account */,
  "account_number" TEXT /* Account number associated with the energy consumption data */,
  "account_name" TEXT /* Name of the account holder */,
  "account_type" TEXT /* Type of account (e.g., residential, commercial, industrial) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the account */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("account_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_consumption" (
  "consumption_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy consumption record */,
  "account_id" INTEGER /* ID of the account associated with the energy consumption data */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the energy consumption */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the day (0-23) when the energy was consumed */,
  "kwh" REAL /* Energy consumption in kWh */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of energy (e.g., grid, solar, wind) */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the energy consumed */,
  "is_peak" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the consumption occurred during peak hours */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("consumption_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_consumption_account_id FOREIGN KEY ("account_id") REFERENCES accounts ("account_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data record */,
  "consumption_id" INTEGER /* ID of the energy consumption record associated with the weather data */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Fahrenheit */,
  "dew_point" REAL /* Dew point in degrees Fahrenheit */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity as a percentage */,
  "sea_level_pressure" REAL /* Sea level pressure in inches */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Visibility in miles */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Wind direction as a string (e.g., WNW, WSW, West) */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation in inches */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Cloud cover as a percentage */,
  "uv_index" REAL /* UV index */,
  "solar_radiation" REAL /* Solar radiation in W/m² */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_consumption_id FOREIGN KEY ("consumption_id") REFERENCES energy_consumption ("consumption_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy metadata record */,
  "consumption_id" INTEGER /* ID of the energy consumption record associated with the metadata */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the year (1-12) */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the month (1-31) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the energy consumption */,
  "day_of_week" INTEGER /* Day of the week (1-7) as an integer */,
  "weekday" INTEGER /* Weekday (1-7) as an integer, where 1 is Monday and 7 is Sunday */,
  "peak_hour" INTEGER /* Peak hour of the day (0-23) when energy consumption was highest */,
  "season" TEXT /* Season of the year (e.g., spring, summer, fall, winter) */,
  "holiday" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the day is a holiday */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_metadata_consumption_id FOREIGN KEY ("consumption_id") REFERENCES energy_consumption ("consumption_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, energy manager, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
